tasks.description: Phase 1 Data Model: Define PinnedShotGroup type in types/index.ts with fields (shotId, trackId, clipIds[], mode, videoAssetKey?, imageClipSnapshot?). Add optional pinnedShotGroups to TimelineConfig. Add 'pinnedShotGroups' to ALLOWED_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS in serialize.ts, update serializeForDisk to conditionally include it (follow customEffects pattern), ensure validateSerializedConfig allows it. Update migrateToFlatTracks in migrate.ts to preserve pinnedShotGroups in BOTH return paths (lines 167-186 — this function rebuilds config with an allowlist that drops unknown keys).
tasks.executor_notes: Defined `PinnedShotGroup` and snapshot types on `TimelineConfig`, allowed `pinnedShotGroups` in serialization validation/output, and preserved cloned pinned groups in both `migrateToFlatTracks()` return paths. Verified with `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.test.ts`.

tasks.description: Phase 1 Data Threading: Update rowsToConfig in timeline-data.ts to accept optional pinnedShotGroups param and include in returned config. Update materializeData in useTimelineCommit.ts to pass current.config.pinnedShotGroups to rowsToConfig. Verify buildDataFromCurrentRegistry passes config through repairConfig→migrateToFlatTracks→assembleTimelineData (no extra changes needed if T1 is correct). Update render-bounds.validation.test.ts and any other direct rowsToConfig callers. Search for all callers with grep.
tasks.executor_notes: Threaded `pinnedShotGroups` through `rowsToConfig()` and `materializeData()`, updated the direct round-trip caller in `render-bounds.validation.test.ts` to assert pinned-group persistence, and verified `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry()` already preserves the required `repairConfig -> migrateToFlatTracks -> assembleTimelineData` path without further edits. Verified all `rowsToConfig()` callers with grep and ran the affected Vitest modules.

tasks.description: Phase 1 Mutation Model: Add new TimelineEditMutation variant { type: 'pinnedShotGroups', pinnedShotGroups: PinnedShotGroup[] } in useTimelineCommit.ts. Implement the branch in applyEdit: read current from dataRef, create updatedConfig with new pinnedShotGroups, call buildDataFromCurrentRegistry(updatedConfig, current), then commitData(). Add optional pinnedShotGroupsOverride to type:'rows' variant. CRITICAL (correctness-1/correctness-4): For the 'rows' branch, after materializeData() produces newData, if pinnedShotGroupsOverride is set, you must set it on the config THEN rebuild via buildDataFromCurrentRegistry so stableSignature is recomputed. Do NOT just mutate the already-assembled TimelineData — that leaves stableSignature stale.
tasks.executor_notes: Added the new `type: 'pinnedShotGroups'` mutation, added `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` on `type: 'rows'`, and rebuilt from config in the rows override path so `stableSignature` is recomputed from the final config instead of mutating assembled data in place. Also updated the existing `type: 'config'` branch to preserve current `customEffects` and `pinnedShotGroups` during reserialization. Verified with the surrounding hook and save-utils suites.

tasks.description: Phase 1 Validation: Run serialize.test.ts and useShotGroups.test.ts to confirm nothing broken. Add test to serialize.test.ts verifying pinnedShotGroups survives serializeForDisk→validateSerializedConfig round-trip. Run npm run build for type check.
tasks.executor_notes: Added a serialize round-trip test covering `pinnedShotGroups`, reran the full `useShotGroups` module, and verified the batch with `npm run build`. The requested validation suite passed: `serialize.test.ts`, `useShotGroups.test.ts`, the added batch verification modules, and a successful production build.

tasks.description: Phase 2 Inference Suppression: Add isPinned:boolean to ShotGroup interface in useShotGroups.ts. Add optional pinnedShotGroups param. Convert each PinnedShotGroup to ShotGroup with isPinned:true using getShotColor and shot name lookup. Suppress inferred groups using clipIds SUBSET matching (FLAG-001): for each inferred group, suppress if its clipIds are a subset of any pinned group's clipIds. Return merged array: pinned groups + filtered inferred groups. Wire in TimelineEditor.tsx: pass data?.config.pinnedShotGroups to useShotGroups call (~line 327).
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `useShotGroups` to surface pinned groups, suppress inferred groups via clip-id subset matching, and preserve prior behavior when `pinnedShotGroups` is undefined. Verified with the full `useShotGroups` test module, the surrounding hook suite, and a successful build.

tasks.description: Phase 2 Visual Distinction: Add isPinned to PositionedShotGroup in TimelineCanvas.tsx. Populate from ShotGroup.isPinned in positionedShotGroups useMemo. Update border rendering (~lines 758-769): suggested groups get border-dashed, pinned keep border-solid. Use existing color-mix pattern (no hardcoded colors). Show shot name label always for pinned groups (not just on hover).
tasks.executor_notes: Verified `TimelineCanvas` now carries `isPinned` into positioned shot groups, renders suggested groups with dashed lighter overlays, keeps pinned groups solid with the stronger existing color-mix treatment, and keeps pinned shot labels visible without hover. Validation passed in the full `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module and the production build.

tasks.description: Phase 2 Tests: Verify all 8 existing useShotGroups tests pass unchanged. Add 4 new tests: (1) pinned group suppresses inferred group whose clipIds are a subset, (2) pinned group does NOT suppress different clipIds cluster of same shot, (3) pinned groups appear with isPinned:true, (4) mixed pinned+inferred merge correctly. Run npm run build.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified all 8 existing `useShotGroups` tests still pass, added the 4 required suppression and merge cases for pinned groups, and confirmed the production build still succeeds. Validation passed in the full `useShotGroups.test.ts` module, the surrounding timeline editor modules, and the production build.

tasks.description: Phase 3 usePinnedShotGroups Hook: Create src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts. Implement pinGroup(shotId, trackId, clipIds), unpinGroup(shotId, trackId), updatePinnedGroup(shotId, trackId, updates). All mutations read current pinnedShotGroups from dataRef.current.config, compute new array, call applyEdit({ type: 'pinnedShotGroups', pinnedShotGroups }). Never directly mutate dataRef.current (conventions-2).
tasks.executor_notes: Created `usePinnedShotGroups` with `pinGroup`, `unpinGroup`, and `updatePinnedGroup`, each reading current pinned groups from config and committing through `applyEdit({ type: 'pinnedShotGroups', pinnedShotGroups })`. Source verification confirms the hook only reads `dataRef.current` and performs all writes through `applyEdit`.

tasks.description: Phase 3 Context Menu: Add isPinned, trackId, mode to ShotGroupMenuState in ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx. Add callback props onPinGroup, onUnpinGroup, onSwitchToImages. Add menu items: 'Pin as Shot Group' (when !isPinned), 'Unpin' (when isPinned), 'Switch to Final Video' (when hasFinalVideo && (!isPinned || mode==='images')), 'Switch to Images' (when isPinned && mode==='video'). Preserve existing finalVideoMap guard and callback signature. Update openShotGroupMenu calls in TimelineCanvas.tsx to include isPinned, trackId, mode. Wire callbacks in TimelineEditor.tsx via usePinnedShotGroups hook.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the shot-group context menu now carries pinned state, track id, and mode through the canvas/editor wiring; shows pin, unpin, switch-to-video, and switch-to-images actions under the required guards; and preserves the existing switch-to-final-video callback shape. Validation passed in the full `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` and `TimelineEditor.test.ts` modules plus the production build.
commands_run: rg -n "Pin as Shot Group|Unpin|Switch to Final Video|Switch to Images|trackId|isPinned|mode === 'video'" src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx

tasks.description: Phase 4 Clip Lifecycle: Implement reconcilePinnedGroups as an INLINE step within materializeData or the commit path — NOT as a post-commit follow-up applyEdit (addresses callers-1/correctness-5/issue_hints-2). After rows are materialized, compare pinned group clipIds against actual clips in the new rows. Remove stale clipIds, remove empty groups. If video mode and video clip deleted: check imageClipSnapshot, restore if available (include in same edit), else remove group. This runs within the same commit boundary — no extra history/save event.
tasks.executor_notes: Moved pinned-group reconciliation into `materializeData()` so stale clip pruning and deleted-video snapshot restoration happen before commit assembly. Added hook-level tests that cover pinned image deletion and deleted-video restoration; the deletion test records exactly one `beforeCommit` emission, verifying the reconcile path stays inside a single commit boundary.

tasks.description: Phase 4 Switch-to-Video Refactor: Modify useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts. (1) Auto-pin: if not already pinned, create PinnedShotGroup with mode:'images'. (2) Snapshot: save image clip metadata into imageClipSnapshot [{clipId, meta, assetKey}] before swap. (3) Swap to video: remove image clips, insert video clip. Update PinnedShotGroup: mode:'video', videoAssetKey, clipIds:[videoClipId]. Use applyEdit({ type:'rows', pinnedShotGroupsOverride }). (4) Add switchToImages: read imageClipSnapshot, remove video clip, restore image clips. Update PinnedShotGroup to mode:'images'. Single applyEdit each direction. Preserve hasFinalVideo guard and finalVideo.id dismissal flow.
tasks.executor_notes: Refactored `useSwitchToFinalVideo` to auto-pin image groups, snapshot image clip metadata before swap, switch back from stored snapshots, and commit both directions as single `rows` edits with `pinnedShotGroupsOverride`. Verified with dedicated hook tests, source checks, and a successful build.

tasks.description: Phase 5 Grouped Drag: In useClipDrag.ts, on pointer-down (~line 100) record whether clip belongs to a pinned group (store ref). Do NOT expand draggedClipIds yet (FLAG-003). On first pointermove past DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX (~line 250), expand draggedClipIds to include all pinned group clipIds, build clipOffsets. Use existing planMultiDragMoves/applyMultiDragMoves. CRITICAL (correctness-2/3/6, all_locations-1/4): After drag commit, if clips moved cross-track, update PinnedShotGroup.trackId. For the 'rows' path, use pinnedShotGroupsOverride. For the 'config' path (new-track creation via buildConfigFromDragResult), inject pinnedShotGroups into the resolved config before applyEdit. Also check useTimelineTrackManagement.moveClipToRow() for single-clip cross-track moves and update trackId there too.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified pinned-group drag expansion happens only after the drag threshold is crossed, and pinned group `trackId` stays synchronized in the same edit across grouped rows moves, grouped config/new-track moves, and `moveClipToRow()` track moves. Validation passed in the full drag, track-management, and multi-drag test modules plus the production build.

tasks.description: Phase 6 Shot Drop DnD Type: Add 'shot' to DragType union in src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts. Add ShotDropData interface {shotId, shotName, imageGenerationIds[]}. Add setShotDragData/getShotDropData functions with MIME type 'application/x-shot'. Update getDragType() to detect new MIME type. Find shot list/gallery components that render individual shots, add draggable attribute and onDragStart calling setShotDragData with drag preview.
tasks.executor_notes: Added the shared `shot` drag type and MIME helpers, plus native shot-card drag start wiring in `SortableShotItem` that emits shot id/name/non-video image generation ids and reuses the existing drag preview. Verified with `npx vitest run src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDrop.test.ts` and source checks on the shot drag wiring.

tasks.description: Phase 6 Shot Drop Handler: Update onTimelineDragOver in useExternalDrop.ts to accept 'shot' drag type. Add handleShotDrop handler. CRITICAL (callers-2/conventions-1): Do NOT directly call handleAssetDrop — it's side-effectful and commits per asset. Instead, extract a pure clip-builder helper (or build clips inline) that produces the row mutations without committing. For each shot image: register asset, build clip meta with sequential timing. Create PinnedShotGroup with all clipIds, mode:'images'. Use single applyEdit({ type:'rows', pinnedShotGroupsOverride }) for atomic creation.
tasks.executor_notes: Implemented shot-drop handling in `useExternalDrop` by resolving shot images from `ShotsContext`, registering each image asset, building clip mutations through extracted asset-drop helpers, and committing the result as one `type:'rows'` edit with `pinnedShotGroupsOverride`. The external-drop test verifies a single atomic edit and confirms `handleAssetDrop` is not called for shot payloads.

tasks.description: Phase 7 Auto-Sync: Create usePinnedGroupSync hook or add to usePinnedShotGroups. Watch ShotsContext for changes to shots that have pinned groups on the timeline. When images added to a shot with pinned group in mode:'images': register new image assets, create clips with duration matching average of existing group clips, insert at correct position, update PinnedShotGroup clipIds. When images removed: remove corresponding clips, update clipIds. Use applyEdit({ type:'rows', pinnedShotGroupsOverride }) per sync. Guard: only sync when mode==='images'. Debounce to avoid rapid-fire edits from realtime events.
tasks.executor_notes: Added debounced pinned-group image sync in `usePinnedShotGroups`, wired it into `TimelineEditor`, and constrained sync to image-mode groups with all edits routed through `applyEdit({ type:'rows', pinnedShotGroupsOverride })`. Verified with hook tests covering debounce and video-mode guard, plus the full batch validation modules and build.

tasks.description: Phase 8 Final Validation: Run all tests. Verify: (1) all 8 existing useShotGroups tests pass unchanged, (2) serialize round-trip test with pinnedShotGroups passes, (3) new suppression tests pass, (4) npm run build succeeds with no type errors, (5) render-bounds.validation.test.ts compiles. Grep for any remaining rowsToConfig callers. Inspect supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/db.ts to confirm permissive 'as TimelineConfig' cast means extra keys pass through. Run full video-editor test suite.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the full `src/tools/video-editor/` suite passes, including the original and new `useShotGroups` coverage, the serialize round-trip coverage, and `render-bounds.validation.test.ts`. Confirmed `npm run build` succeeds in this batch. Also verified `rowsToConfig()` still has only the expected callers and confirmed the AI timeline-agent load path still relies on the permissive `TimelineConfig` cast via `normalizeTimelineRow()`.

watch_items: CRITICAL — stableSignature divergence (correctness-1/correctness-4): pinnedShotGroupsOverride in the 'rows' branch MUST be applied to the config BEFORE rebuilding TimelineData via buildDataFromCurrentRegistry/assembleTimelineData. Do NOT post-hoc mutate an already-assembled TimelineData object — stableSignature would be stale.

CRITICAL — double history on clip delete (issue_hints-2/callers-1/correctness-5): reconcilePinnedGroups MUST be inline in the same commit boundary as the triggering edit, NOT a separate follow-up applyEdit. Each applyEdit creates its own undo entry and save scheduling pass.

CRITICAL — cross-track trackId staleness (correctness-2/3/6, all_locations-1/4, scope-1): When grouped drag or moveClipToRow() moves clips to a different track, PinnedShotGroup.trackId must be updated in the SAME applyEdit. This affects THREE paths: (a) multi-drag same-row via type:'rows', (b) multi-drag new-track via type:'config' with buildConfigFromDragResult, (c) single-clip moveClipToRow in useTimelineTrackManagement.

CRITICAL — grouped drag timing (FLAG-003): Do NOT expand draggedClipIds on pointer-down. Only expand after drag threshold is crossed. Pointer-down without threshold crossing should be a normal click-selection.
migrateToFlatTracks has TWO return paths (lines 167-186) — BOTH must include pinnedShotGroups. Missing one silently drops pinned groups on config migration (FLAG-005).

Shot drop clip creation (callers-2/conventions-1): handleAssetDrop is side-effectful and commits per asset. For atomic multi-clip shot drop, extract a pure builder or build clips inline — do NOT call handleAssetDrop in a loop.
Plan Steps 13-14 are TODO placeholders that duplicate Steps 21-24 (plan-execution-1/all_locations-3). The finalize maps both to the concrete shot-drop and auto-sync tasks.
All pinned group mutations go through applyEdit, never direct mutation of dataRef.current (conventions-2). This preserves undo/redo history.
Existing useShotGroups tests pass without pinnedShotGroups param — the param must default to undefined with identical behavior.
customEffects pattern in serialize.ts, migrate.ts, and rowsToConfig is the template for pinnedShotGroups threading.
Auto-sync (Phase 7) should be debounced to avoid rapid-fire applyEdit calls from realtime shot image events.
DEBT watch: Do not worsen clip-drag-scroll-sync, registry-patch-testing, or undo-reconciliation debt items.

sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed `Switch to Final Video` only appears when `finalVideoMap.get(shotId)` is present and the group is not already in video mode, `Switch to Images` only appears when `mode === 'video'`, and the existing `onSwitchToFinalVideo({ shotId, clipIds, rowId })` callback shape is preserved.

sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed reconciliation is inline in `materializeData()` with no follow-up `applyEdit`, and the new hook-level deletion test records exactly one `beforeCommit` emission for a pinned-clip deletion while the deleted-video test restores the image snapshot in the same commit.

sense_checks.question: Does switch-to-video snapshot image clips BEFORE removing them? Does switch-to-images restore from snapshot? Both as single applyEdit calls with pinnedShotGroupsOverride?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed `switchToFinalVideo` snapshots image clip metadata before removing clips, `switchToImages` restores from the stored snapshot, and both paths commit as single `applyEdit({ type: 'rows', pinnedShotGroupsOverride })` edits.

sense_checks.question: Is group expansion deferred to after drag threshold (not pointer-down)? Does cross-track drag update trackId in the same applyEdit? Are ALL three move paths covered (rows, config/new-track, moveClipToRow)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed pinned-group expansion is deferred until the first pointer move past the drag threshold, and pinned group `trackId` is updated in the same edit across all required move paths: rows multi-drag, config/new-track multi-drag, and `moveClipToRow()`.

sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed shot drop uses one `applyEdit({ type: 'rows', ... pinnedShotGroupsOverride })` call, and the external-drop test verifies `handleAssetDrop` is not called for shot payloads.

sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed auto-sync is limited to `mode === 'images'`, debounced with `window.setTimeout`, and writes through a single `applyEdit` rows mutation with `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` rather than mutating timeline state directly.

meta_commentary:
Execution guidance:

1. **Start with T1+T2 sequentially** — they're the foundation. Read types/index.ts, serialize.ts, migrate.ts, timeline-data.ts, and useTimelineCommit.ts before making changes. The customEffects pattern is your template.

2. **T3 is the highest-risk task.** The 'rows' branch with pinnedShotGroupsOverride is where critique flags converge. Key insight: after materializeData() produces new data from rows, you MUST set pinnedShotGroups on the resulting config and THEN rebuild TimelineData from it (or ensure assembleTimelineData sees the final config). Post-hoc mutation of an already-assembled TimelineData leaves stableSignature stale. The cleanest approach: materializeData returns a config, apply override to config, then pass to buildDataFromCurrentRegistry.

3. **T10 (clip lifecycle)** is second highest risk. The plan says "post-commit reconcile" but that creates double history. Instead: make reconciliation part of materializeData itself. When it rebuilds rows, check if any pinned group clipIds reference clips no longer in the new rows. Clean them in the same data pass, before commitData. Zero extra undo entries.

4. **T12 (grouped drag)** has THREE code paths to handle: (a) same-track multi-drag via type:'rows' — add pinnedShotGroupsOverride with updated trackId, (b) new-track multi-drag via buildConfigFromDragResult + type:'config' — inject pinnedShotGroups into the resolved config, (c) single-clip moveClipToRow in useTimelineTrackManagement — needs similar treatment. Read all three paths before coding.

5. **T14 (shot drop)** — the existing handleAssetDrop is imperative and commits per asset. You MUST extract clip-building logic into a pure function or build clips inline. Do NOT call handleAssetDrop in a loop — that violates the single-applyEdit requirement.

6. **T13 (shot drag type) has no dependencies** and can start in parallel with Phase 1.

7. **T15 (auto-sync)** needs debouncing. Realtime shot image events can fire rapidly. Consider a 300ms debounce or batching within a single requestAnimationFrame.

8. **Plan has duplicate steps**: Steps 13-14 are TODO placeholders that duplicate Steps 21-24. This finalize maps both sets to the concrete T13/T14/T15 tasks. Ignore the TODO versions.

9. **Test strategy**: Don't create new test files except within existing test files. Add tests to useShotGroups.test.ts and serialize.test.ts. The final validation (T16) runs full video-editor suite + build check.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Define PinnedShotGroup type and add to TimelineConfig
Step 2: Add new mutation type to TimelineEditMutation
Step 3: Thread pinnedShotGroups through serialization
Step 4: Thread pinnedShotGroups through migration
Step 5: Thread pinnedShotGroups through data assembly
Step 6: Thread through commit cycle
Step 7: Update rowsToConfig callers and tests
Step 8: Verify persistence round-trip
Step 9: Update useShotGroups to support pinned groups
Step 10: Wire pinnedShotGroups into useShotGroups call
Step 11: Visual distinction in TimelineCanvas
Step 12: Create usePinnedShotGroups hook
Step 13: Shot drop TODO placeholder (maps to concrete Steps 21-23)
Step 14: Auto-sync TODO placeholder (maps to concrete Step 24)
Step 15: Update ShotGroupContextMenu
Step 16: Update context menu opening in TimelineCanvas
Step 17: Wire callbacks in TimelineEditor
Step 18: Handle clip deletion in pinned groups
Step 19: Refactor useSwitchToFinalVideo for pinned groups
Step 20: Implement grouped drag for pinned groups
Step 21: Add shot drag type to shared DnD utilities
Step 22: Add drag source in shot list components
Step 23: Handle shot drops on timeline
Step 24: Subscribe to shot image changes for pinned groups
Step 25: Unit tests for useShotGroups
Step 26: Serialization tests
Step 27: Build and full test suite

validation.completeness_notes: All 27 plan steps are mapped to tasks. Steps 13-14 (TODO placeholders) are mapped to the same concrete tasks as Steps 21-24 (their non-placeholder equivalents). Steps 1/3/4 consolidated into T1 (same files). Steps 5/6/7 consolidated into T2 (data threading). Steps 15/16/17 consolidated into T9 (context menu wiring). This reflects the plan's own grouping of related small steps.
